articles.title: SAP S/4HANA Work Process Saturation — Period-End

sections.title: Diagnosis
sections.content: Check SM50/SM66 for WP utilisation. Identify dialog vs background WP class allocation via SM37. Threshold: >80% dialog WP = risk of saturation during peak.

sections.title: Immediate Relief
sections.content: Reschedule batch jobs from dialog to background WP class via SM37. Execute SM04 session cleanup for stale sessions. ZeroOps executes automatically for risk score < 25.

sections.title: Prevention
sections.content: Configure ZeroOps automated WP class validation to fire 30 minutes before period-end batch windows. Set alert threshold at 70% (warning) and 85% (critical).

sections.title: Escalation
sections.content: If WP pool exceeds 95% and autonomous remediation fails, engage BASIS team. Potential for emergency dialog WP extension requires BASIS admin.

sections.title: Diagnosis
sections.content: Check EDI gateway connection pool — expect 0/N active partners after network maintenance. Queue depth will be growing. Message age alert confirms stale queue.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.content: Execute sequential partner reconnection (not bulk — v3.2.1 defect). ZeroOps automated reconnection handles this. Once connections established, replay messages in chronological order.

sections.title: Prevention
sections.content: Permanent fix in CHG-4821 (AI SDLC pipeline). Interim: add ZeroOps post-maintenance reconnection rule for 02:00–03:00 window.

sections.title: Diagnosis
sections.content: Check disk utilisation on all Jenkins agents via Datadog. Retention policy config drift common after patching cycles. Agent-level policy vs global policy conflict.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.content: ZeroOps automated cleanup removes artefacts >14 days with no active references. Ansible playbook enforces retention policy across all agents.

sections.title: Prevention
sections.content: ZeroOps daily disk check on all Jenkins agents at 06:00. Alert at 80%, automated cleanup at 85%. Config compliance check weekly via Ansible.

articles.title: SoD Conflict — AP Approval + Vendor Master

sections.title: Conflict Detail
sections.content: AP invoice approval (AP_APPROVER) combined with vendor master edit (VENDOR_MASTER_EDIT) creates payment cycle and vendor fraud SoD violation. Regulatory requirement: must not coexist without compensating controls.

sections.title: Compensating Controls
sections.content: AP transaction limit (recommend €25K), dual-control for payments above threshold, quarterly access review, exception logged in GRC.

sections.title: Escalation
sections.content: Finance Director or delegated Change Manager must approve. GRC risk register entry required. Annual re-certification.
